risk: Repeated higher-dose paracetamol use may increase INR and bleeding risk; occasional use is generally lower risk than NSAIDs.
mechanism: The interaction appears dose- and duration-dependent and may involve interference with the vitamin K cycle; the exact mechanism is not fully established.
recommendation: Prefer the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration. If repeated use, especially around or above 2 g/day for several days, is needed, notify the anticoagulation team and consider closer INR monitoring.
monitoring: INR when repeated use is clinically relevant
urgency_changers: Active bleeding, melena, hematemesis, hematuria, severe headache, syncope, or hemodynamic instability.
pharmacist_traps: Do not describe paracetamol as completely interaction-free with warfarin.
